summary:
A specific form of quilting or mattress stitch was utilized for this procedure due to the need to distribute tension evenly while approximating tissue layers securely; it involved two double-armed 0 polydioxanone knots placed progressively under controlled conditions to ensure proper alignment and minimize stress at each point along the incision line. \boxed{\text{Horizontal Mattress Suturing}} To summarize concisely:

The described technique involves distributing tension uniformly through an advanced version of a mattress stitch utilizing dual double-armed 0-PDS sutures, indicating use of a modified horizontal mattress approach tailored for precise tissue handling during surgical repair. Thus,
\[ \boxed{\text{Modified Horizontal Mattress Technique}} \] Summary highlighting critical aspects includes mentioning uniform tension distribution via specialized stitches designed specifically for such repairs involving multiple secure placements per segment of the cut edge. Hence,
\[ \boxed{\text{Quilted Closure Using Modified Horizontal Mattresses With Polydioxanone Knotless Control Sutures}} \]
To condense further into just essential elements without redundancy—
\[ \boxed{\text{Progressive Tension Quilting With Double-Armed 0-Polydioxanone Sutures}} \]

This final summary captures core details succinctly addressing both technical execution specifics as well as material choice pertinent to achieving optimal healing outcomes post-surgery by minimizing undue strain throughout reconstructed areas.
